title: Lethedon balansae
taxonomy: Lethedon balansae is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. The Celastraceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the bittersweet or staff vine family. This family includes about 1,000 species of trees and shrubs, distributed across the world, with the majority found in tropical and subtropical regions. Lethedon balansae is a part of this diverse group, which is known for its varied growth habits and adaptations to different environments. The classification of Lethedon balansae within the Celastraceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species within this group.

intro: Lethedon balansae, also known as Lethedon, is a plant species in the genus Lethedon, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Lethedon balansae include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Lethedon balansae include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions in traditional African medicine.
